title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 4 (Book 2)
source: Practice Tests CPE 2, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-18, read the three texts below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Faraday and Lighthouses

Faraday was (1) ______ adviser to Trinity House in 1836, a job he held for almost 30 years. By the 1830s, he had already made the greatest (2) ______ of his career — including the principle of the electric motor, the transformer and the dynamo, work that (3) ______ the way for the development of technologies based on electricity.

The research he did for Trinity House was far less exciting. Over the years, he experimented with different oils, looking for one that would burn brighter and more (4) ______. He checked out new designs of lamps and made improvements to the optics. Lighthouse keepers had a lot of problems with water. One which constantly arose was the amount of condensation that ran down the windows of the room at the top of the tower that (5) ______ the oil lamps. Condensation reduced the intensity of the light beam and in cold weather the water froze, (6) ______ the light still more.

Earth's Oceans

Astronomers may have taken a step towards understanding the origins of Earth's oceans — thanks to a (7) ______ comet. All the evidence suggests that when the Earth was formed it was too hot to (8) ______ water. So, astronomers (9) ______ that the oceans came from comets bombarding Earth shortly after its formation. However, (10) ______ show that these icy celestial objects contained too much 'heavy' water to be (11) ______ for Earth's oceans. Further progress was not made until comet Linear fell to pieces during late July, 2000 and released more than three billion kilograms of water into space. Chemical analyses suggest this comet probably contained the right elements for Earth's oceans. Astronomers are now searching for more comets like Linear to (12) ______ their ideas.

Cadet School

The Director nodded, 'Yes. Well, we've (13) ______ our system over a long period of time, and we've found that home visits just don't fit into the picture until the cadet is thoroughly oriented to our (14) ______ of doing things. We say a year merely as a general guide. Sometimes it's longer than that. Parents can visit here at (15) ______ times.' The Director (16) ______ enquiringly at Mr Holston, who tried to think of some more questions but could not.

'Actually,' the Director continued, 'the cadets seem to prefer it this way, once they get started. What we're (17) ______, Mr Holston, is to motivate them to achieve success, which means success in becoming a fully oriented member of this community and you can see how home visits might cause a little (18) ______ in the process.'

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'appointed adviser' = được bổ nhiệm làm cố vấn. 'Appoint' là động từ chuẩn dùng cho việc bổ nhiệm vào một vị trí/chức vụ. Bẫy: 'applied' (A) nghĩa là ứng tuyển (chủ động), không phù hợp ngữ cảnh bị động. 'positioned' (B) mang nghĩa vật lý. 'allotted' (D) nghĩa là phân bổ (thời gian, tài nguyên). Chuyên đề: Collocations — appoint as/to.
questions.answerLocation: Faraday was appointed adviser

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'made the greatest breakthroughs' = tạo ra những bước đột phá lớn nhất. 'Breakthrough' = bước tiến quan trọng trong nghiên cứu. Bẫy: 'breakdowns' (B) nghĩa là sự hỏng hóc/suy sụp. 'breakouts' (C) nghĩa là vượt ngục. 'breakaways' (D) nghĩa là sự ly khai/tách ra. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — compound nouns with 'break'. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: made the greatest breakthroughs of his career

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'paved the way for' = mở đường cho, dọn đường cho. Đây là collocation cố định: 'pave the way for the development of technologies' = mở đường cho sự phát triển công nghệ. Bẫy: 'set' (A) cần 'the stage for' không phải 'the way for'. 'established' (B) không đi với 'the way'. 'made' (C) cần 'way' không có 'the' — 'made way for' có nhưng khác nghĩa. Chuyên đề: Collocations — pave the way for.
questions.answerLocation: work that paved the way for the development

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'burn more efficiently' = cháy hiệu quả hơn. 'Efficiently' nói về việc sử dụng tài nguyên (dầu) một cách tối ưu — cháy sáng mà ít tốn dầu. Bẫy: 'competently' (B) và 'capably' (C) dùng cho người (có năng lực), không phù hợp cho việc đốt dầu. 'effectually' (D) rất ít dùng và mang nghĩa 'có hiệu lực pháp lý'. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — adverbs of efficiency. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: burn brighter and more efficiently

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'housed the oil lamps' = chứa đèn dầu. 'House' (động từ) = chứa đựng, lưu giữ bên trong. 'The room that housed the oil lamps' = căn phòng chứa đèn dầu. Bẫy: 'encased' (A) nghĩa là bao bọc chặt (trong vỏ). 'sheltered' (C) nghĩa là che chở (khỏi thời tiết). 'accommodated' (D) thường dùng cho người. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — house (verb). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: the room that housed the oil lamps

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'dimming the light' = làm mờ ánh sáng. 'Dim' là từ chuyên dùng cho ánh sáng (làm mờ đi). Bẫy: 'fading' (A) thường dùng cho màu sắc/âm thanh dần biến mất. 'lessening' (B) dùng cho số lượng/mức độ nói chung. 'shading' (D) nghĩa là che bóng, không phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Collocations — dim the light.
questions.answerLocation: dimming the light still more

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'a disintegrating comet' = sao chổi đang tan rã. 'Disintegrate' = vỡ ra thành nhiều mảnh nhỏ. Phù hợp vì sau đó bài nói 'comet Linear fell to pieces'. Bẫy: 'decomposing' (A) nghĩa là phân hủy (sinh học). 'dismantling' (C) nghĩa là tháo rời (có chủ đích). 'disentangling' (D) nghĩa là gỡ rối. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — dis- prefix verbs. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: a disintegrating comet

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'too hot to hold water' = quá nóng để giữ nước. 'Hold' dùng cho việc chứa đựng/giữ lại chất lỏng. Bẫy: 'enclose' (A) nghĩa là bao quanh/đóng kín. 'embody' (C) nghĩa là hiện thân/thể hiện (trừu tượng). 'carry' (D) nghĩa là mang theo, nhưng 'hold water' là collocation tự nhiên hơn trong ngữ cảnh khoa học. Chuyên đề: Collocations — hold water.
questions.answerLocation: too hot to hold water

questions.instruction: Gap (9):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'proposed that' = đề xuất/giả thuyết rằng. 'Astronomers proposed that the oceans came from comets' = các nhà thiên văn đề xuất giả thuyết rằng đại dương đến từ sao chổi. 'Propose' dùng cho giả thuyết khoa học. Bẫy: 'recommended' (B) nghĩa là khuyến nghị (cho hành động). 'stipulated' (C) nghĩa là quy định (trong hợp đồng). 'specified' (D) nghĩa là xác định cụ thể. Chuyên đề: Academic vocabulary — propose a theory. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: astronomers proposed that the oceans came from comets

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'studies show that' = các nghiên cứu cho thấy. 'Studies' là từ chuẩn trong ngữ cảnh khoa học để chỉ các công trình nghiên cứu. Bẫy: 'examinations' (A) thường chỉ bài kiểm tra. 'reviews' (B) là bài đánh giá tổng quan. 'dissertations' (C) là luận văn dài. Chuyên đề: Academic vocabulary — studies.
questions.answerLocation: studies show that these icy celestial objects

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'responsible for' = chịu trách nhiệm cho/là nguyên nhân của. 'Too much heavy water to be responsible for Earth's oceans' = quá nhiều nước nặng để là nguồn gốc đại dương Trái Đất. 'Responsible for' dùng cho nguyên nhân gây ra. Bẫy: 'liable' (B) dùng cho trách nhiệm pháp lý. 'answerable' (C) nghĩa là phải chịu trách nhiệm trước ai đó. 'accountable' (D) cũng mang nghĩa pháp lý. Chuyên đề: Collocations — responsible for.
questions.answerLocation: to be responsible for Earth's oceans

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'confirm their ideas' = xác nhận ý tưởng/giả thuyết. 'Confirm' là từ chuẩn trong khoa học khi nói về việc kiểm chứng giả thuyết. Bẫy: 'ratify' (A) dùng cho hiệp ước/thỏa thuận (phê chuẩn). 'certify' (C) dùng cho chứng nhận chính thức. 'resolve' (D) nghĩa là giải quyết. Chuyên đề: Academic vocabulary — confirm. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: searching for more comets to confirm their ideas

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'worked out our system' = xây dựng/phát triển hệ thống. 'Work out' = phát triển, hoàn thiện qua thời gian. 'We've worked out our system over a long period' = chúng tôi đã xây dựng hệ thống qua thời gian dài. Bẫy: 'written out' (A) nghĩa là viết ra đầy đủ. 'phased out' (C) nghĩa là loại bỏ dần. 'brought out' (D) nghĩa là xuất bản/giới thiệu. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — work out. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: we've worked out our system

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'our way of doing things' = cách làm việc của chúng tôi. 'Way of doing things' là collocation cực kỳ phổ biến. Bẫy: 'usage' (A) nghĩa là cách sử dụng (ngôn ngữ/đồ vật). 'process' (B) nghĩa là quy trình — 'process of doing' không tự nhiên. 'custom' (D) nghĩa là phong tục — 'custom of doing things' không chuẩn. Chuyên đề: Collocations — way of doing things.
questions.answerLocation: oriented to our way of doing things

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'at specific times' = vào những thời điểm cụ thể. 'Specific' nghĩa là cụ thể, xác định — phụ huynh chỉ được thăm vào những thời điểm nhất định. Bẫy: 'distinctive' (B) nghĩa là đặc biệt/nổi bật. 'characteristic' (C) nghĩa là đặc trưng. 'distinguishing' (D) nghĩa là để phân biệt. Các từ B, C, D đều mang nghĩa 'khác biệt/đặc biệt' nhưng không phù hợp ngữ cảnh 'thời gian quy định'. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — specific vs. distinctive.
questions.answerLocation: visit here at specific times

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'gazed enquiringly at' = nhìn chăm chú với vẻ dò hỏi. 'Gaze' = nhìn chăm chú, thường trung tính hoặc tích cực. Bẫy: 'leered' (A) nghĩa là nhìn với vẻ dâm dục/gian xảo. 'ogled' (C) nghĩa là nhìn chằm chằm (thèm thuồng). 'gaped' (D) nghĩa là há hốc mồm nhìn (kinh ngạc). Chỉ 'gazed' phù hợp với 'enquiringly' (dò hỏi). Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — verbs of looking. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: gazed enquiringly at Mr Holston

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'what we're looking for is to motivate them' — thực chất đây là cấu trúc cleft: 'What we're looking for' = Điều chúng tôi mong muốn/tìm kiếm. 'Look for' = tìm kiếm, mong muốn. Bẫy: 'standing for' (A) nghĩa là đại diện cho/chịu đựng. 'hunting for' (C) nghĩa là săn lùng — quá mạnh. 'making for' (D) nghĩa là hướng tới. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs — look for.
questions.answerLocation: what we're looking for is to motivate them

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'cause a little disruption' = gây ra một chút gián đoạn. 'Disruption' = sự gián đoạn, phá vỡ. Phù hợp ngữ cảnh: việc thăm nhà có thể gây gián đoạn quá trình hội nhập. Bẫy: 'distortion' (A) nghĩa là sự bóp méo/xuyên tạc. 'disorganisation' (B) nghĩa là sự thiếu tổ chức. 'distinction' (D) nghĩa là sự phân biệt. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — disruption.
questions.answerLocation: cause a little disruption in the process

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-15, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each space. Use only one word in each space.
sections.passageHtml:
All Work and No Play

Universally, work has been a central focus point (0) IN society. As old as the idea of work (1) ______ is the question of what constitutes 'real work'. This is, in fact, a very subjective question indeed. (2) ______ you to ask a miner, or any labourer for that matter, what real work is, he would probably reply that real work entails working (3) ______ your hands and, in the process, getting them dirty.

To the average blue-collar worker, white-collar workers are those people who sit in their offices day (4) ______ day doing little or (5) ______ in the line of actual work. By (6) ______, if you approached a white-collar worker or a professional of (7) ______ sort with the same question, you can rest assured that they (8) ______ adamantly maintain that the world would stop revolving (9) ______ their invaluable intellectual contribution to the scheme (10) ______ things.

This idea is reflected (11) ______ the vocabulary used to describe work and its related subjects. Words (12) ______ career, vocation and profession carry a (13) ______ elevated connotation than the simple term 'job'. The (14) ______ three lexical items convey the idea of learned persons sitting at desks and using their grey matter to solve matters involving financial, legal or medical matters, (15) ______ the humble slave away at some mundane work station or assembly line task.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap (1): As old as the idea of work ______ is the question
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ITSELF. 'As old as the idea of work itself' = cũ như chính ý tưởng về công việc. 'Itself' là đại từ phản thân dùng để nhấn mạnh. Chuyên đề: Reflexive pronouns — emphasis. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: As old as the idea of work itself

questions.instruction: Gap (2): ______ you to ask a miner... he would probably reply
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WERE. 'Were you to ask' = Nếu bạn hỏi (câu điều kiện loại 2 đảo ngữ). Đây là dạng formal/literary của 'If you were to ask'. Đảo ngữ: 'Were + subject + to + infinitive'. Chuyên đề: Inversion — conditional (were + subject + to). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: Were you to ask a miner

questions.instruction: Gap (3): real work entails working ______ your hands
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WITH. 'working with your hands' = làm việc bằng tay. 'Work with one's hands' = lao động chân tay. Giới từ 'with' chỉ phương tiện/công cụ. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — with (instrument).
questions.answerLocation: working with your hands

questions.instruction: Gap (4): sit in their offices day ______ day
questions.explanation: Đáp án: AFTER (hoặc BY). 'day after day' = ngày qua ngày. Đây là cấu trúc lặp cố định chỉ sự liên tục: 'noun + after/by + noun'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — day after day.
questions.answerLocation: day after day doing little

questions.instruction: Gap (5): doing little or ______ in the line of actual work
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NOTHING. 'little or nothing' = ít hoặc không gì cả. Đây là fixed expression rất phổ biến: 'do little or nothing' = hầu như không làm gì. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — little or nothing. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: doing little or nothing in the line of actual work

questions.instruction: Gap (6): By ______, if you approached a white-collar worker
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CONTRAST. 'By contrast' = ngược lại, trái lại. Dùng để chuyển sang quan điểm đối lập (từ blue-collar sang white-collar). Chuyên đề: Linking expressions — by contrast. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: By contrast, if you approached a white-collar worker

questions.instruction: Gap (7): a professional of ______ sort
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ANY (hoặc SOME). 'of any sort' = thuộc bất kỳ loại nào. 'A professional of any sort' = một chuyên gia thuộc bất kỳ lĩnh vực nào. Chuyên đề: Determiners — any/some + sort. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: a professional of any sort

questions.instruction: Gap (8): they ______ adamantly maintain
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WOULD. 'they would adamantly maintain' = họ sẽ kiên quyết khẳng định. 'Would' dùng trong câu điều kiện loại 2 (hệ quả của 'if you approached'). Chuyên đề: Modal verbs — would (conditional). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: they would adamantly maintain

questions.instruction: Gap (9): the world would stop revolving ______ their invaluable contribution
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WITHOUT. 'without their invaluable intellectual contribution' = nếu không có sự đóng góp trí tuệ vô giá của họ. 'Without' diễn tả điều kiện thiếu vắng. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — without.
questions.answerLocation: stop revolving without their invaluable intellectual contribution

questions.instruction: Gap (10): the scheme ______ things
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OF. 'the scheme of things' = trật tự sự vật, hệ thống vận hành. 'In the grand scheme of things' là idiom phổ biến. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — scheme of things.
questions.answerLocation: the scheme of things

questions.instruction: Gap (11): This idea is reflected ______ the vocabulary
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IN. 'reflected in' = được phản ánh trong. 'This idea is reflected in the vocabulary' = ý tưởng này được phản ánh trong từ vựng. Collocation cố định: 'reflected in'. Chuyên đề: Prepositions — reflected in.
questions.answerLocation: reflected in the vocabulary

questions.instruction: Gap (12): Words ______ career, vocation and profession
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LIKE (hoặc SUCH — nhưng cần 'such as'). 'Words like career, vocation' = những từ như career, vocation. 'Like' dùng để đưa ví dụ. Nếu dùng 'such' thì cần 'such as'. Chuyên đề: Exemplification — like/such as.
questions.answerLocation: Words like career, vocation and profession

questions.instruction: Gap (13): carry a ______ elevated connotation than
questions.explanation: Đáp án: MORE. 'a more elevated connotation than' = một ý nghĩa cao sang hơn. 'More + adjective + than' là cấu trúc so sánh hơn. 'Elevated' (tính từ dài) cần 'more' để so sánh. Chuyên đề: Comparatives — more + long adjective.
questions.answerLocation: a more elevated connotation than the simple term 'job'

questions.instruction: Gap (14): The ______ three lexical items
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FORMER (hoặc FIRST). 'The former three lexical items' = ba thuật ngữ đã nêu trước đó (career, vocation, profession). 'Former' chỉ cái đã đề cập trước. Nối với 'the humble' (worker) ở phần sau tạo sự đối lập. Chuyên đề: Reference words — former.
questions.answerLocation: The former three lexical items convey the idea

questions.instruction: Gap (15): ______ the humble slave away at some mundane task
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WHILE (hoặc WHILST/WHEREAS). 'while the humble slave away' = trong khi những người lao động bình thường cặm cụi. 'While' tạo sự đối lập giữa 'learned persons' và 'the humble'. Chuyên đề: Conjunctions — while (contrast). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: while the humble slave away

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 16-25, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
Pop Musicals

Lord Andrew Lloyd Webber, a man whose (0) compositions, eclectic rock based (COMPOSE) works, helped (16) ______ British and American (17) ______ theatre (VITAL / MUSIC) in the late 20th century.

As a student at Oxford University, a (18) ______ was founded between Webber and Timothy Rice (PARTNER) to put on dramatic productions. Their first (19) ______ successful venture was 'Joseph and The Amazing (NOTE) Technicolor Dreamcoat', a pop oratorio for children that earned world-wide acclaim.

This was followed by the rock opera, 'Jesus Christ Superstar', an extremely popular, though (20) ______ work that blended classical forms to tell the story of (CONTROVERSY) Christ's last days. His last major collaboration with Rice was on 'Evita'. 'Cats' was his next major production, in which he set to music verses from a children's book by T.S. Eliot.

With two (23) ______, Charles Hart and Richard Stilgoe, he then composed a hugely successful version (COLLABORATE) of 'The Phantom of the Opera'.

Lloyd Webber's best works were flashy spectacles that featured vivid melodies, forceful and dramatic staging. He was able to blend such varied and (24) ______ genres as rock and roll, English music-hall song, and (SIMILAR) (25) ______ forms into music that had a wide mass appeal. (OPERA)

questionGroups.instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct word form.

questions.instruction: Gap (16): works helped ______ British and American theatre (VITAL)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: REVITALISE (từ gốc: VITAL). Thêm tiền tố 're-' và hậu tố '-ise' để tạo động từ. 'Helped revitalise theatre' = giúp hồi sinh sân khấu. 'Revitalise' = mang lại sức sống mới. Cần động từ vì đứng sau 'helped'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Verbs (re- + root + -ise).
questions.answerLocation: helped revitalise British and American theatre

questions.instruction: Gap (17): British and American ______ theatre (MUSIC)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: MUSICAL (từ gốc: MUSIC). Thêm hậu tố '-al' để tạo tính từ. 'Musical theatre' = nhạc kịch. Đây là collocation rất phổ biến. Cần tính từ vì đứng trước danh từ 'theatre'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-al).
questions.answerLocation: British and American musical theatre

questions.instruction: Gap (18): a ______ was founded between Webber and Rice (PARTNER)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: PARTNERSHIP (từ gốc: PARTNER). Thêm hậu tố '-ship' để tạo danh từ. 'A partnership was founded' = một sự hợp tác được thành lập. Cần danh từ vì đứng sau mạo từ 'a' và là chủ ngữ của 'was founded'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ship).
questions.answerLocation: a partnership was founded

questions.instruction: Gap (19): Their first ______ successful venture (NOTE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NOTABLY (từ gốc: NOTE). Thêm hậu tố '-ably' để tạo trạng từ. 'Notably successful' = thành công đáng chú ý. Cần trạng từ vì bổ nghĩa cho tính từ 'successful'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adverbs (-ably).
questions.answerLocation: first notably successful venture

questions.instruction: Gap (20): though ______ work that blended classical forms (CONTROVERSY)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CONTROVERSIAL (từ gốc: CONTROVERSY). Thêm hậu tố '-al' để tạo tính từ (bỏ '-y' thêm '-ial'). 'Though controversial' = mặc dù gây tranh cãi. Cần tính từ vì bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'work'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-ial).
questions.answerLocation: though controversial work

questions.instruction: Gap (21): (word formation question 21)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LYRICIST (từ gốc: LYRIC). Thêm hậu tố '-ist' để tạo danh từ chỉ người. 'Lyricist' = người viết lời bài hát. Rice là lyricist nổi tiếng làm việc với Lloyd Webber. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ist for person).
questions.answerLocation: lyricist

questions.instruction: Gap (22): (word formation question 22)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ADAPTATION (từ gốc: ADAPT). Thêm hậu tố '-ation' để tạo danh từ. 'Adaptation' = sự chuyển thể. Lloyd Webber chuyển thể nhiều tác phẩm cho sân khấu. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ation).
questions.answerLocation: adaptation

questions.instruction: Gap (23): With two ______, Charles Hart and Richard Stilgoe (COLLABORATE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: COLLABORATORS (từ gốc: COLLABORATE). Thêm hậu tố '-or' và '-s' (số nhiều) để tạo danh từ chỉ người. 'Two collaborators' = hai cộng sự. Charles Hart và Richard Stilgoe là hai cộng sự. Cần danh từ số nhiều vì 'two'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Nouns (-ors for people).
questions.answerLocation: With two collaborators, Charles Hart and Richard Stilgoe

questions.instruction: Gap (24): such varied and ______ genres (SIMILAR)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DISSIMILAR (từ gốc: SIMILAR). Thêm tiền tố phủ định 'dis-' để tạo tính từ. 'Varied and dissimilar genres' = các thể loại đa dạng và khác biệt. Cần tính từ phủ định vì ngữ cảnh nhấn mạnh sự khác biệt giữa các thể loại (rock, music-hall, opera). Bẫy: quên thêm tiền tố 'dis-'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Negative adjectives (dis-).
questions.answerLocation: varied and dissimilar genres

questions.instruction: Gap (25): ______ forms into music (OPERA)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OPERATIC (từ gốc: OPERA). Thêm hậu tố '-tic' để tạo tính từ. 'Operatic forms' = các hình thức opera. Cần tính từ vì đứng trước danh từ 'forms'. Chuyên đề: Word Formation — Adjectives (-tic/-atic).
questions.answerLocation: operatic forms into music

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 32-39, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
sections.passageHtml:
Key Word Transformation

32. The plans for a new sports centre are unlikely to go ahead unless the cost is reduced. (QUESTION)

The plans for a new sports centre are ______ unless the cost is reduced.

33. Lack of adequate collateral may prevent the bank from giving him a loan. (STAND)

Lack of adequate collateral may ______ him a loan.

34. The authorities should have done something about the problem earlier. (HIGH)

It is ______ about the problem.

35. I'm sure the children were pleased to see their grandparents again. (MUST)

The children ______ their grandparents again.

36. The company's failure was entirely due to the owner's mismanagement. (BLAME)

The owner's mismanagement was entirely to ______ the company's failure.

37. Nobody can deny that she has a talent for painting. (QUESTION)

Her talent for painting is ______ .

38. I don't think you have been entirely honest with me. (WHOLE)

I don't think you have told ______ .

39. The fact that he had been in prison came to light only after his death. (KNOWN)

It ______ been in prison until after his death.

questionGroups.instruction: Fill in the gap for each sentence below using 3 to 8 words.

questions.instruction: Sentence 32: The plans for a new sports centre are ______ unless the cost is reduced. (Key word: QUESTION)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'in question'. 'in question' = bị nghi ngờ, chưa chắc chắn. 'The plans are in question' = Các kế hoạch đang bị đặt dấu hỏi / chưa chắc chắn. Câu gốc 'unlikely to go ahead' ≈ 'in question'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — in question. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: plans are in question unless the cost is reduced

questions.instruction: Sentence 33: Lack of adequate collateral may ______ him a loan. (Key word: STAND)
acceptedAnswers: stand in the way of the bank giving
stand in the way of giving
questions.correctAnswer: stand in the way of the bank giving
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'stand in the way of the bank giving'. 'stand in the way of' = cản trở, ngăn cản. 'Lack of collateral may stand in the way of the bank giving him a loan' = Thiếu tài sản thế chấp có thể cản trở việc ngân hàng cho anh ta vay. Câu gốc 'prevent from giving' ≈ 'stand in the way of giving'. Chuyên đề: Idioms — stand in the way of.
questions.answerLocation: stand in the way of the bank giving him a loan

questions.instruction: Sentence 34: It is ______ about the problem. (Key word: HIGH)
acceptedAnswers: high time the authorities did something
questions.correctAnswer: high time the authorities did something
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'high time the authorities did something'. 'It is high time' + past simple = đã đến lúc phải... (thể hiện sự chậm trễ). 'It is high time the authorities did something about the problem' = Đã đến lúc chính quyền phải làm gì đó về vấn đề này. Bẫy: sau 'it is high time' dùng PAST SIMPLE (subjunctive), không phải present. Chuyên đề: Grammar — it is high time + past simple.
questions.answerLocation: high time the authorities did something about the problem

questions.instruction: Sentence 35: The children ______ their grandparents again. (Key word: MUST)
acceptedAnswers: must have been pleased to see
must have been delighted to see
questions.correctAnswer: must have been pleased to see
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'must have been pleased to see'. 'must have been' = chắc hẳn đã... (suy luận chắc chắn về quá khứ). 'The children must have been pleased to see their grandparents' = Bọn trẻ chắc hẳn đã vui khi gặp lại ông bà. Câu gốc 'I'm sure' ≈ 'must have'. Chuyên đề: Modal verbs — must have + past participle (deduction). Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: must have been pleased to see their grandparents

questions.instruction: Sentence 36: The owner's mismanagement was entirely to ______ the company's failure. (Key word: BLAME)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'blame for'. 'be to blame for' = phải chịu trách nhiệm cho. 'The mismanagement was entirely to blame for the company's failure' = Sự quản lý tồi hoàn toàn là nguyên nhân gây ra thất bại. Lưu ý: 'to blame' (infinitive) không phải 'to be blamed' — đây là cấu trúc đặc biệt. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — be to blame for.
questions.answerLocation: was entirely to blame for the company's failure

questions.instruction: Sentence 37: Her talent for painting is ______ . (Key word: QUESTION)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'beyond question'. 'beyond question' = không thể nghi ngờ, hiển nhiên. 'Her talent for painting is beyond question' = Tài năng hội họa của cô ấy là không thể phủ nhận. Câu gốc 'Nobody can deny' ≈ 'beyond question'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — beyond question. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: Her talent for painting is beyond question

questions.instruction: Sentence 38: I don't think you have told ______ . (Key word: WHOLE)
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'me the whole truth'. 'the whole truth' = toàn bộ sự thật. 'I don't think you have told me the whole truth' = Tôi không nghĩ bạn đã nói cho tôi toàn bộ sự thật. Câu gốc 'not been entirely honest' ≈ 'not told the whole truth'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions — the whole truth. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: have told me the whole truth

questions.instruction: Sentence 39: It ______ been in prison until after his death. (Key word: KNOWN)
acceptedAnswers: was not known that he had
questions.correctAnswer: was not known that he had
questions.explanation: Đáp án: 'was not known that he had'. 'It was not known that' = không ai biết rằng. 'It was not known that he had been in prison until after his death' = Không ai biết ông ta từng ở tù cho đến sau khi ông qua đời. Câu gốc 'came to light only after' ≈ 'was not known until after'. Chuyên đề: Passive structures — it was not known that. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: was not known that he had been in prison until after his death

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read four extracts about precious stones and an article about advertising. For questions 19-26 and 34-40, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Diamond

It is perhaps difficult to believe that diamond, like graphite and charcoal, is a form of carbon. Diamond crystallises in cubic form, at enormous pressures and high temperatures over the course of millions of years. The process has been imitated under laboratory conditions and then applied industrially to create artificial diamonds. These have proven to be mainly of either industrial, plain quality or very small in size. The diamond's exceptional properties arise from the crystal structure, in which the bonding between the carbon atoms is immensely strong and uniform.

First, a diamond possesses extraordinary powers of light reflection. When properly cut, it gathers light within itself, reflecting it back in a shower of fire and brilliance. Second, it is the only gem mineral composed of a single, unadulterated element, making it the purest of earth's gemstones. Thirdly, it is the hardest transparent substance known to man. The only material that can cut diamond is... another diamond!

An Unusual Stone

In the latter part of the 19th century, the jewellery profession was strictly confined to precious stones. No so-called fancy stones were on sale in any jewellery store in the country. Yet, reviewing the beautiful minerals that I had gathered during my mineralogical investigations, it seemed to me that many women would be happy to array themselves in the endless gorgeous colours of these gems.

So one day, armoured in youthful enthusiasm, I wrapped a tourmaline in a bit of gem paper, and all the way to my destination rehearsed my arguments. I was finally received by the managing head of what was even then the largest jewellery establishment in the world and showed him my drop of green light. I explained — a very little; the gem itself was its own best argument. Tiffany bought it. The cheque, which crinkled in my pocket as I walked home, meant very little in comparison with the fact that I had interested the foremost jeweller in my revolutionary theory and made the acquaintance of a man who was later to become my close friend.

Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend

Composed of pure carbon, the rock is not the rarest of gems but found in abundance the world over. However, every time one of the stones is purchased, a credulous public is buying into a cunning marketing campaign conceived by the De Beers' cartel. After the Great Depression, Sir Ernest Oppenheimer sent his son Harry from Johannesburg to New York. The objective was to transform America's taste for small low-quality stones into one which would open its wallets to the higher-quality gems no longer selling in Europe.

Into the hands of the N. W. Ayer advertising agency came the challenge. 'A Diamond is Forever' was the slogan used to establish a tradition aimed at capturing hearts and loosening purse strings. Diamond rings became the best way to declare one's eternal love. Having permeated American culture, De Beers set out to eventually manipulate the perceptions of the rest of the world.

Jade

The track leads down to a small encampment. As the sun's first glow appears, its aura illuminates a Yunnan trader preparing for the day ahead. With a watchmaker's precision, all the possessions in his earthly world are gathered together and placed into burlap sacks. He sets off in the misty light just before dawn, for the rock on the mule's back is no ordinary stone. Burma's jade mines are on the brink of discovery.

In the same remote corner of the planet, yesteryear's idyll is today broken. Across the naked earth crawl thousands of human ants, prying boulders loose from the compact brown soil. The operation is a study in patience. The construction of Egypt's Great Pyramids was a similar study in patience but with one important difference. That in Upper Burma consists of deconstruction, the dismantling of entire mountains, one pebble at a time. Patience, patience — those who hurry lose, they miss something, they don't go to heaven. Those who hurry don't find jade.

Advertising and Children

The average citizen is bombarded with TV commercials, posters and newspaper advertisements wherever he goes. The amount of advertising to which we are exposed is phenomenal, yet advertisers are being hurt by their industry's worst recession in a decade and a conviction that advertising can go no further. Despite the ingenuity of the advertisers, the consumer has become increasingly cynical and simply blanks out all but the subtlest messages. The advertising industry has therefore turned to a more vulnerable target: the young.

Advertisers acknowledge that the commercial pressures of the 1990s had an extraordinary effect on childhood: it is now generally believed that the cut-off point for buying toys has been falling by one year every five years. In effect, the result is the premature ageing of children.

Increasingly low budgets have left schools vulnerable to corporate funding and sponsorship schemes. While on the face of it this would seem to be a purely philanthropic gesture on the part of the companies concerned, the other side of the coin is a pervasive commercial presence in the classroom.

This marked shift in advertising perceptions also means that a great deal of supposedly adult advertising has an infantile appeal. Children obviously cannot buy these things for themselves; what is behind these advertisements is more subtle. Advertisers have come to recognize that if children can successfully pester their parents to buy them the latest line in trainers, then they can also influence their parent's choice of car or credit card.

There are many who would argue that advertising has little influence on children, who are exposed to such a huge variety of visual images that advertisements simply become lost in the crowd. Rather, they would argue that it is the indulgent parents who boost sales figures. In fact, the advertising industry itself admits that since peer pressure plays such an important role in children's lives, they are not difficult to persuade.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

questions.instruction: 19. What gives diamond its special qualities?
options: A. Its ability to withstand division.
B. The fact that it consists of carbon.
D. The way it is cut.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu then chốt: 'The diamond's exceptional properties arise from the crystal structure, in which the bonding between the carbon atoms is immensely strong and uniform.' — Tính chất đặc biệt của kim cương đến từ CẤU TRÚC TINH THỂ (crystal structure) = 'inherent composition' (thành phần/cấu tạo bẩm sinh). Bẫy: B hấp dẫn vì đề cập carbon, nhưng than chì cũng là carbon mà không có tính chất giống kim cương — nên là CẤU TRÚC, không chỉ thành phần hóa học. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail.
questions.answerLocation: exceptional properties arise from the crystal structure

questions.instruction: 20. The writer considers diamonds as symbols of love due to
options: B. the fact that they are natural stones.
C. the fact that they cannot be easily changed.
D. their being difficult to make.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Câu cuối: 'It is fitting that the purest and most brilliant of all the world's stones should also be the most enduring one. These qualities make a diamond the perfect symbol of engagement and love.' — 'purest', 'most brilliant', 'most enduring' = ĐẶC TÍNH VẬT LÝ (physical characteristics) làm kim cương trở thành biểu tượng tình yêu. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: A. their physical characteristics.

questions.instruction: 21. Why does the writer say he 'explained — a very little'?
options: A. He was too nervous to say much.
B. His concise arguments persuaded the jeweller.
C. The stone's potential was obvious to the jeweller.
D. The jeweller didn't have time to spare.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu tiếp: 'the gem itself was its own best argument' = viên đá tự nó là lập luận tốt nhất. Tức là viên đá ĐẸP ĐẾN MỨC không cần giải thích nhiều — tiềm năng của nó HIỂN NHIÊN với nhà kim hoàn. Bẫy: A hấp dẫn vì 'rehearsed my arguments' gợi ý lo lắng, nhưng 'armoured in youthful enthusiasm' cho thấy tự tin. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: the gem itself was its own best argument

questions.instruction: 22. What does the writer consider his greatest achievement that day?
options: A. He had profited from the sale of his humble gem.
B. He had developed an important relationship.
C. He had sold his gem to a conservative jeweller.
D. He had successfully challenged a bias.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Câu then chốt: 'meant very little in comparison with the fact that I had interested the foremost jeweller in my revolutionary theory' — Tiền bán không quan trọng bằng việc ông đã thu hút nhà kim hoàn hàng đầu QUAN TÂM đến lý thuyết CÁCH MẠNG của mình. 'Revolutionary theory' = lý thuyết đá quý 'fancy stones' có thể bán được → thách thức THÀNH KIẾN (bias) của ngành. Bẫy: B hấp dẫn vì ông đề cập 'close friend' nhưng đó là KẾT QUẢ sau này, không phải THÀNH TỰU LỚN NHẤT hôm đó. Chuyên đề: Reading — main achievement.
questions.answerLocation: interested the foremost jeweller in my revolutionary theory

questions.instruction: 23. De Beers' objective was to
options: A. change Americans' attitudes towards buying presents.
B. inform the public of a diamond's rarity.
C. create a need for more expensive stones.
D. ensure that all types of people were in possession of a diamond.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu then chốt: 'The objective was to transform America's taste for small low-quality stones into one which would open its wallets to the higher-quality gems no longer selling in Europe.' — Mục tiêu là BIẾN ĐỔI sở thích từ đá rẻ sang đá ĐẮT hơn = 'create a need for more expensive stones'. Bẫy: A hấp dẫn nhưng quá rộng — không chỉ về quà tặng. B sai vì bài nói kim cương 'found in abundance' (không hiếm). Chuyên đề: Reading — purpose.
questions.answerLocation: transform America's taste for small low-quality stones into higher-quality gems

questions.instruction: 24. Which word in the text implies that De Beers' campaign was successful?
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'enduring advertising campaign' — 'enduring' nghĩa là LÂU BỀN/kéo dài. Một chiến dịch quảng cáo 'enduring' (bền vững qua thời gian) ngụ ý rằng nó THÀNH CÔNG (vì nếu thất bại thì đã bị bỏ). Bẫy: 'cunning' (A) chỉ nói về sự xảo quyệt, không chỉ thành công. 'loosening' (D) nói về mở hầu bao nhưng đó là MỤC ĐÍCH, không phải KẾT QUẢ. Chuyên đề: Reading — vocabulary implication.
questions.answerLocation: C. enduring.

questions.instruction: 25. The discovery of Burma's jade mines was
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'into history... into immortality... Burma's jade mines are on the brink of discovery' — từ ngữ 'history', 'immortality' cho thấy đây là SỰ KIỆN LỊCH SỬ (historical event). Bẫy: A không có bằng chứng — người thương nhân có chủ đích. C không phù hợp ngữ cảnh đoạn đầu. D đề cập ở đoạn sau nhưng không phải ý chính. Chuyên đề: Reading — inference.
questions.answerLocation: into history... into immortality... jade mines are on the brink of discovery

questions.instruction: 26. According to the writer, jade mining
options: B. leads to a virtuous life.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Câu then chốt: 'The operation is a study in patience' và 'dismantling of entire mountains, one pebble at a time' — đào jade là quá trình CỰC KỲ VẤT VẢ (laborious), đòi hỏi sự kiên nhẫn phi thường. Bẫy: D hấp dẫn vì 'don't go to heaven' nhưng đó là ẩn dụ cho thất bại, không phải trải nghiệm tâm linh. B cũng liên quan 'heaven' nhưng không phải ý chính. Chuyên đề: Reading — main idea.
questions.answerLocation: The operation is a study in patience... dismantling of entire mountains

questions.instruction: 34. What does the writer say about advertising in the first paragraph?
options: A. Capitalism has led to the demise of advertising.
B. We should have a cynical view of advertisers.
C. Advertising is facing new challenges these days.
D. The industry has run out of new ideas.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Câu then chốt: 'advertisers are being hurt by their industry's worst recession in a decade and a conviction that advertising can go no further' và 'the consumer has become increasingly cynical'. Ngành quảng cáo đang đối mặt với THÁCH THỨC MỚI: suy thoái + người tiêu dùng hoài nghi. Bẫy: D hấp dẫn nhưng quá tuyệt đối. A cũng quá tuyệt đối ('demise'). Chuyên đề: Reading — main idea.
questions.answerLocation: advertisers are being hurt by their industry's worst recession

questions.instruction: 35. The bombardment of advertisements has led to
options: A. children taking more notice of them.
B. greater difficulty in attracting consumers' attention.
D. people being less likely to spend money.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'the consumer has become increasingly cynical and simply blanks out all but the subtlest messages' — Người tiêu dùng ngày càng hoài nghi và lọc bỏ mọi thông điệp trừ những thông điệp tinh tế nhất → KHÓ HƠN để thu hút sự chú ý. Chuyên đề: Reading — cause and effect.
questions.answerLocation: consumer has become increasingly cynical and simply blanks out

questions.instruction: 36. How have children changed during the past decade?
options: B. They are growing up more quickly.
D. They are not playing as much.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'the cut-off point for buying toys has been falling by one year every five years... the result is the premature ageing of children' — Trẻ em LỚNLÊN NHANH HƠN ('premature ageing' = già trước tuổi). Bẫy: D gần đúng nhưng chỉ là hệ quả, không phải bản chất thay đổi. A quá rộng. Chuyên đề: Reading — detail.
questions.answerLocation: the result is the premature ageing of children

questions.instruction: 37. What does the writer imply in the third paragraph?
options: A. Advertising agencies need to preserve their reputations.
B. Schools welcome aid from big business.
C. There are restrictions on how financial aid may be used.
D. Companies expect nothing in return for their help.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Câu then chốt: 'Increasingly low budgets have left schools vulnerable to corporate funding' — Ngân sách thấp khiến trường học DỄ BỊ TỔN THƯƠNG trước tài trợ doanh nghiệp = trường CHÀO ĐÓN viện trợ vì thiếu tiền. Tuy nhiên 'the other side of the coin is a pervasive commercial presence' — có mặt trái. Bẫy: D sai hoàn toàn vì bài nói rõ 'the other side of the coin' là hiện diện thương mại. Chuyên đề: Reading — implication.
questions.answerLocation: low budgets have left schools vulnerable to corporate funding

questions.instruction: 38. How have children changed the face of advertising?
options: A. Children are influencing the purchases of adult products.
B. They are now the advertising industry's sole market.
C. More products have to be sold to children.
D. Children have become more selective in their choices.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Câu then chốt: 'if children can successfully pester their parents to buy them the latest line in trainers, then they can also influence their parent's choice of car or credit card, and so children become an advertising tool in themselves.' — Trẻ em ẢNH HƯỞNG đến việc mua SẢN PHẨM NGƯỜI LỚN (car, credit card). Chuyên đề: Reading — detail.
questions.answerLocation: they can also influence their parent's choice of car or credit card

questions.instruction: 39. What does the writer suggest in the last paragraph?
options: A. Adults feel increasingly threatened by advertising.
B. Children are unlikely to be influenced by their friends.
C. Parents avoid spending too much money on their children.
D. Children have a less sheltered existence than they used to.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Câu then chốt: 'children, far from being in some sense protected from the myriad of pressures... should now be exposed to this influence in all aspects of their lives' — Trẻ em KHÔNG ĐƯỢC BẢO VỆ (less sheltered) như trước, phải đối mặt với mọi áp lực. Bẫy: B ngược ý — bài nói 'peer pressure plays such an important role'. C không có bằng chứng. Chuyên đề: Reading — writer's suggestion.
questions.answerLocation: D. Children have a less sheltered existence than they used to.

questions.instruction: 40. In the text as a whole, the writer's purpose is to
options: A. explain the inspiration for advertisements.
B. expose the exploitation of children.
C. deter parents from giving in to advertisers.
D. prevent advertisers from infiltrating schools.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Toàn bộ bài viết nói về cách ngành quảng cáo nhắm vào trẻ em: 'more vulnerable target: the young', 'premature ageing', 'pervasive commercial presence in the classroom', 'children become an advertising tool'. Mục đích chính là PHƠI BÀY sự BÓC LỘT trẻ em (expose the exploitation). Bẫy: C và D chỉ là một phần nhỏ, không phải mục đích tổng thể. A không phù hợp vì bài phê phán, không chỉ giải thích. Chuyên đề: Reading — overall purpose.
questions.answerLocation: turned to a more vulnerable target: the young

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from an article about rock paintings. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (27-33). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Bradshaw Rock Paintings

All eyes are on the shaman: arms outstretched, head back, her face hidden behind a mask. She wears a long, tapering cap, and clutches a short wand in each hand. There are tassels at her hips and elbows, and these jump as she begins to move. The crowd around her watches, spellbound, as she embarks upon her journey to the spirit world.

[Gap 27]

The suggestion that this rock art may be the oldest known depiction of a shamanistic ritual comes from a group of researchers led by Per Michaelsen. They argue that these ancient paintings may represent not only early religious practices but perhaps also a cultural heritage common to all humans.

[Gap 28]

Michaelsen estimates that there may be as many as 100,000 Bradshaw 'galleries' tucked under rock overhangs along the region's major river systems. Many of the paintings have never been studied. But observations made over the past few decades reveal several distinct artistic styles.

[Gap 29]

The subjects' dress changes over time, as does the style of the paintings themselves. The next oldest figures are notable for the sashes around their midriffs.

[Gap 30]

There are also so-called elegant action figures of people running and hunting, which are difficult to date because none is superimposed on a painting from another period.

[Gap 31]

In 1997, one group used luminescence dating to assign a minimum age of 17,000 years. The second study used radiocarbon dating and came up with a much younger estimate: about 4,000 years.

[Gap 32]

Either way, the Bradshaw people were not the original inhabitants of the Kimberley. Archaeological evidence suggests the first settlers arrived at least 40,000 years ago.

[Gap 33]

Intriguingly, one of the Bradshaw paintings shows a boat with upswept prow and stern, and multiple paddlers. It raises the possibility that the artists were originally a seafaring people.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph you do not need to use.

questions.instruction: Gap 27:
questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn G giải thích đây là cảnh được ghi lại trên đá: 'That is one possible interpretation of a scene recorded thousands of years ago on a remote rocky outcrop in north-west Australia. The painting is part of a vast collection that opens a window on an ancient hunter-gatherer society.' Đoạn trước mô tả cảnh shaman — đoạn G tiết lộ đây thực ra là BỨC TRANH ĐÁ, chuyển từ miêu tả sống động sang ngữ cảnh khảo cổ. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — revealing context.
questions.answerLocation: one possible interpretation of a scene recorded thousands of years ago

questions.instruction: Gap 28:
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Đoạn B giới thiệu tên gọi và địa điểm tranh Bradshaw: 'The Bradshaws, as the paintings are collectively known, were first noted by Europeans in 1891 and take their name from Joseph Bradshaw. They are found in a region known as the Kimberley, a remote place even by Australian standards.' Nối phần nghiên cứu với mô tả 100,000 galleries ở đoạn sau. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — naming & location.
questions.answerLocation: The Bradshaws were first noted by Europeans in 1891

questions.instruction: Gap 29:
questions.explanation: Đáp án F. Đoạn F mô tả loại hình cổ nhất — tasseled figures: 'The oldest and largest paintings are known as the tasseled figures. They are the most realistic of the images and show figures in static poses in what appears to be ceremonial dress.' Phù hợp vì đoạn sau nói 'The next oldest figures' — cần đoạn mô tả figures CỔ NHẤT trước. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — chronological sequence.
questions.answerLocation: The oldest and largest paintings are known as the tasseled figures

questions.instruction: Gap 30:
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn C mô tả clothes-peg figures: 'Younger than the sash and tasseled figures, the clothes-peg figures are much more highly stylised. The older paintings tend to show profiles, but these are frontal portraits. Gone is the anatomical detail.' Tiếp nối sash figures ở đoạn trước, mô tả loại trẻ hơn tiếp theo. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — chronological progression.
questions.answerLocation: Younger than the sash and tasseled figures, the clothes-peg figures

questions.instruction: Gap 31:
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn E chuyển sang vấn đề niên đại: 'All these paintings provide a wealth of detail about the material culture of the Bradshaw people. Yet, despite this, nobody knows when the Bradshaw culture developed. Only two groups have attempted to date the paintings directly.' Nối elegant action figures với hai nghiên cứu niên đại ở đoạn sau. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — transition to dating.
questions.answerLocation: nobody knows when the Bradshaw culture developed

questions.instruction: Gap 32:
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn A giải thích tranh cãi giữa hai nhà nghiên cứu: 'Both researchers stand by their results. Roberts suspects that Watchman's radiocarbon samples might have been contaminated. Watchman thinks that the painting dated by Roberts was pre-Bradshaw.' Nối hai kết quả khác biệt ở đoạn trước với 'Either way' ở đoạn sau. Chuyên đề: Gapped text — dispute resolution.
questions.answerLocation: Both researchers stand by their results

questions.instruction: Gap 33:
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Đoạn D mô tả Kỷ Băng Hà ảnh hưởng đến khu vực: 'The Ice Age brought cooler temperatures, strong winds and lower rainfall. The sea level dropped... The coastline was as much as 400 kilometres further to the north-west. Australia was connected by land to New Guinea.' Nối từ 'first settlers arrived 40,000 years ago' → thay đổi khí hậu → dẫn đến câu hỏi về nguồn gốc Bradshaw ở đoạn sau (boat painting). Chuyên đề: Gapped text — environmental context.
questions.answerLocation: The Ice Age brought cooler temperatures... Australia was connected to New Guinea

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: As a result, the impact on the instrumental curriculum and the measures used to assess progress through it will be such that they will need to adapt to maintain their relevance for a broader sector of the population. Ultimately, they will need to encompass a wider range of musical skills.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: For all these reasons, there is likely to be a continuing demand for instrumental teaching in the short term. What about the longer term picture? Is there likely to be a shift in focus and, if so, what direction will it take?

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: I would respond to the latter question on a positive note. The music industry is one of the major generators of income in Britain and musical skill and talent will continue to be important in preparing individuals to work in a variety of professions, in particular those related to the media.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: This represents a fundamental change from traditional practice and it will be accompanied by the need to respond to demands for public accountability. Viewed positively, this should provide an opportunity for all those involved in music education to demonstrate the high quality of music tuition available.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: There seems little doubt that the widening access to music is likely to continue, fulfilling as it does so many human needs. On a national level, no major state occasion is without music. For individuals, it provides opportunities for numerous activities, formal and informal.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: One of the best-recognised functions is that of providing an outlet for emotional expression. Its influence on our moods can be therapeutic. It provides a means of communicating which goes beyond words and provides us with shared unspoken understandings.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: Developing in parallel with this trend is a likely increase in the number of people, across the whole age range, who wish to actively participate in music making. Such activities are likely to be community based and will reflect the musical traditions of that community whatever they may be.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: While these results are still to be successfully replicated, other data from Europe has indicated that an increase in group music lessons can have positive effects on social relationships in school and on concentration in young children and those with behavioural difficulties.
